bullets.text: Full-year CR 95.3, meeting the ≤96.0 target despite severity outpacing models in H2; Q1 CR was 89.3 but deteriorated to 100.5 in June as driving returned to pre-pandemic levels and loss costs accelerated sharply.

Personal Lines NPW crossed $36.2 billion milestone (+$3B YOY); Commercial Lines reached $8.0 billion NPW (+51% YOY) achieving 7th consecutive year of double-digit CL premium growth; Protective Insurance acquisition expanded fleet/affinity capabilities.
Personal Lines ended 2021 with 22.7 million PIFs, adding more than 1.3 million policies; Robinson (auto+home bundle) customers grew ~13%; 21% of customers have multiple Progressive products.

Took 'larger bites' of rate increases in H2 2021 as severity accelerated in step-function fashion; advertising spend cut to reduce expenses until adequate pricing restored; personal auto model 8.7 in 22 states (>50% of premium) improving preferred-risk conversion.

Severity outpaced models, especially H2; driving frequency returned to near pre-pandemic levels; prior accident-year development ended at 0% for full year; strong actuarial reserving accuracy cited as a competitive advantage for pricing.

Investment portfolio exceeded $50 billion; total return 2.6% (equity +27%, fixed income slightly negative); $500M 3.75% notes paid off at maturity; $1.50/share variable dividend plus $0.40/share quarterly dividends; share repurchases when below fair value.

BOP expanded to 34 states (>60% of CL multiperil market), adding 17 states in 2021; commercial model 8.0 with new policy admin system in 19 states (~50% of CL premium); property 4.0/4.1 models in 16 states; Protective Insurance acquisition completed.

Gallup engagement in 97th percentile; hired 13,000+ employees in 2021; people of color in senior leadership rose from 10% to 16% toward doubling goal; solar array added expected to cut campus carbon emissions 1,500 metric tons/year; carbon-neutral goal by 2025.

Progressive ranked #3 in personal auto (~14% market share vs. 0.6% in 1988); #1 in commercial auto; top-15 homeowners carrier; Commercial Lines CR 88.9 driven by exceptional freight market and TNC recovery.

Personal auto industry facing regulatory scrutiny as carriers sought rate increases after 2020 credits; Progressive advocated for regulatory frameworks that allow adequate rates as a prerequisite for healthy competitive voluntary markets.
